title: Zabelia triflora
intro: Zabelia triflora, also known as Korean boxwood or Korean box holly, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae), native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Korean medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Zabelia triflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Zabelia triflora is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Zabelia triflora is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is native to Asia and is known for its white or pinkish flowers. The plant is often classified as a shrub and is a member of the genus Zabelia. Within the Ericaceae family, Zabelia triflora is part of the subfamily Vaccinioideae. The exact classification of Zabelia triflora can be broken down into the following ranks: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Ericales, family Ericaceae, subfamily Vaccinioideae, tribe Vaccinieae, and genus Zabelia.
